top_3_procedural_failures.failure_type: Inadequate cumulative/connected actions analysis in EIS
top_3_procedural_failures.description: Agency prepared an EIS but failed to adequately analyze connected or cumulative actions, or relied on a prior EIS without reassessing changed circumstances and new information (e.g., BLM's reliance on prior Echanis wind project EIS for transmission line without adequate supplementation/cumulative analysis for sage-grouse habitat).
contributing_agencies: Department of the Interior - Bureau of Land Management

top_3_procedural_failures.failure_type: Improper invocation of NEPA waiver / failure to conduct NEPA where statutory authority contested
top_3_procedural_failures.description: Agency proceeded with major federal action (border barrier construction funded via reprogrammed appropriations) without preparing NEPA analysis, relying on a statutory NEPA waiver the court found inapplicable because the underlying funding/authority was unlawful.
contributing_agencies: Department of Homeland Security - U.S. Customs and Border Protection

top_3_procedural_failures.failure_type: Failure to supplement EIS when new information materially affects environmental analysis
top_3_procedural_failures.description: Agency did not prepare a supplemental EIS despite new information (population/habitat data, connected project changes) that was relevant to environmental concerns and bore on the action or its impacts.
contributing_agencies: Department of the Interior - Bureau of Land Management

failure_to_prepare.description: Agency failed to prepare any NEPA document when one was required
top_agencies: Department of Homeland Security - U.S. Customs and Border Protection

failure_to_supplement.description: Agency failed to supplement an existing EA/EIS when circumstances changed
top_agencies: Department of the Interior - Bureau of Land Management

improper_ce_reliance.description: Agency used a CE when an EA or EIS was required

eis_inadequacy.description: EIS was prepared but found substantively inadequate (missing alternatives, cumulative impacts, etc.)
top_agencies: Department of the Interior - Bureau of Land Management

adjacent_statute_violation.description: Failure under ESA Section 7, CWA Section 404, NHPA Section 106, or other adjacent statutes invoked alongside NEPA
top_triggers: ESA Section 7 consultation gaps for sage-grouse
top_agencies: Department of the Interior - Bureau of Land Management

defensibility_gaps.gap: No enforced supplementation trigger when new significant information emerges post-ROD
defensibility_gaps.fix: Require periodic supplementation review checkpoint capturing new species data, habitat trends, and project scope changes; block tiering to prior EIS without documented supplementation determination per 40 CFR 1502.9(c).

defensibility_gaps.gap: Connected/cumulative actions not systematically scoped when multiple federal approvals cover interdependent projects (generation + transmission + access roads)
defensibility_gaps.fix: Require connected-actions checklist per 40 CFR 1508.25(a)(1); flag when project has adjacent federal approvals within defined geographic/temporal nexus.

defensibility_gaps.gap: Statutory NEPA waiver or categorical bypass asserted without documented legal sufficiency review of underlying funding/authorization
defensibility_gaps.fix: Require OGC sign-off and funding-authority memorandum before allowing 'NEPA waived by statute' status; block project transition to construction phase without this attestation.

defensibility_gaps.gap: ESA Section 7 consultation status not linked to NEPA document finalization
defensibility_gaps.fix: Block FONSI/ROD issuance until ESA consultation status is 'concluded' or 'not required (documented)' for projects in listed-species range.

defensibility_gaps.gap: Land management plan amendments covering listed/candidate species lack documented adequate-protection findings tied to best available science
defensibility_gaps.fix: Require explicit science basis citation and FWS coordination record for species-protective provisions in LMP EIS.

software_validation_rules.trigger: IF process_type == 'EIS' AND record_of_decision_date IS NOT NULL AND (new_species_data_received == TRUE OR connected_action_scope_change == TRUE OR elapsed_years_since_ROD > 5)
software_validation_rules.validation: REQUIRE supplementation_determination_memo = PRESENT AND signed_by = responsible_official
software_validation_rules.error_message: Supplementation review required under 40 CFR 1502.9(c)(1): new information or changed circumstances detected. Document a written determination on whether a Supplemental EIS is required before proceeding with tiered decisions.

software_validation_rules.trigger: IF process_type IN ('EA','EIS') AND project has >=1 related federal approval within 10mi OR shared purpose/timeline
software_validation_rules.validation: REQUIRE connected_actions_analysis = COMPLETE AND cumulative_impacts_section = POPULATED
software_validation_rules.error_message: Connected actions detected (40 CFR 1508.25(a)(1)). You must either scope them together or document independent utility. Cumulative impacts analysis (40 CFR 1508.7) is required.

software_validation_rules.validation: REQUIRE ogc_legal_sufficiency_memo = PRESENT AND funding_authority_citation = VALID AND waiver_statute_cite = PRESENT
software_validation_rules.error_message: Statutory NEPA waiver asserted. Attach OGC legal sufficiency memo confirming (a) valid underlying funding/authorization and (b) applicability of the cited waiver provision. Without this, project cannot advance to implementation.

software_validation_rules.trigger: IF project_location INTERSECTS listed_species_range AND process_type IN ('EA','EIS')
software_validation_rules.error_message: ESA Section 7 consultation is not closed. NEPA decision document cannot be signed until consultation is concluded or a documented 'no effect' determination is on file.

software_validation_rules.trigger: IF project_type INCLUDES 'Land Use or Forest Management Plan' AND species_of_concern IS NOT EMPTY
software_validation_rules.validation: REQUIRE best_available_science_citations >= 1 AND fws_coordination_record = PRESENT AND species_protection_rationale = DOCUMENTED
software_validation_rules.error_message: LMP affecting sensitive species requires documented best-available-science basis and FWS/NMFS coordination record before EIS finalization.
Tiering to a prior EIS older than 5 years requires a documented supplementation review confirming continued validity under 40 CFR 1502.9(c).

4th.dominant_failure: none - agency prevailed on LMP EIS challenge

9th.dominant_failure: Failure to prepare NEPA / inadequate supplementation for connected actions

analysis_notes.data_quality_caveats: Sample size is very small (n=5) and two cases share the same underlying project (border barrier, nepatec_uuid fe8262a6...), with split outcomes driven by distinct legal theories (appropriations vs. NEPA waiver). Failure type inferences are drawn from case captions, keywords, and known published rulings rather than full opinion text; specific regulatory bases should be verified against the opinions before operationalizing rules. Circuit-level loss rates are not statistically reliable at this sample size and should be treated as directional indicators only.
